prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Three plants in a row against a dark earthy background. On the left, a tall slender sapling reaching upward toward a single point of warm light, suggesting growth in length. In the middle, an old gnarled tree trunk seen up close, concentric annual rings faintly visible, suggesting growth in girth. On the right, a tuft of grass freshly cut, with new green blades pushing up from the base. Soft amber dawn light, painterly atmospheric style, dark background, no text, no labels.
alt: Three plants growing in three different ways — one shooting upward, one thickening at the trunk, one regrowing after being cut

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). An onion bulb suspended in a glass jar of water, with a thick tangle of pale white roots emerging from its base and reaching down into the water. The very tips of the roots glow with a soft amber inner light, suggesting hidden growth zones. Dark midnight-blue background, atmospheric water reflections, painterly cinematic style. No text, no labels.
alt: Close-up of a root tip with the apical meristem zone glowing softly, fresh roots emerging from a sprouting onion bulb

prompt: Educational line graph on a dark background (#0a0a0a). X-axis labelled 'Days' from 0 to 7. Y-axis labelled 'Length of roots (cm) from base of bulb' from 0 to 6. Two lines plotted: a smooth blue rising curve labelled 'Jar A' going from (1, 0.2) to (7, 5.8) — growing steadily. A second amber/orange line labelled 'Jar B' growing along with Jar A from days 1 to 4, then on day 4 dropping vertically by 1 cm (the cut), then staying flat horizontally from day 4 to day 7. Light grid lines, white axis labels, white legend in the upper-left corner. Clean technical illustration style, no decorative elements.
alt: Line graph comparing root lengths in Jar A (uncut) and Jar B (cut) over 7 days

prompt: Educational botanical illustration of a young sapling cross-section. The plant has a single shoot rising from soil with a few leaves and a clear shoot tip; below the soil line, a primary root extending downward with a clear root tip. Two zones are highlighted with soft amber halos: one at the very tip of the shoot labelled 'Shoot apical meristem' with a small inset showing the actively dividing cell cluster; one at the very tip of the root labelled 'Root apical meristem' with a similar inset. Soil shown as a soft brown band. Dark background (#0a0a0a). White labels with thin white leader lines. Soft greens for shoot, ivory-white for root, warm brown for soil. Flat 2D textbook illustration style. No photorealism, no shading effects.
alt: Diagram of a young sapling showing locations of apical meristem at root tip and shoot tip

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A close-up of a freshly cut massive tree trunk, viewed from above, showing dozens of concentric annual rings — some wide, some narrow — radiating outward from a small dark centre. The rings are highlighted by warm amber low-angle sunlight. Surrounding wood grain visible. Painterly cinematic style, dark earthy palette, atmospheric haze, no text, no labels.
alt: A massive cross-section of an old tree trunk showing many concentric annual rings, lit by warm low light

prompt: Cross-sectional botanical illustration of a young tree trunk viewed from above. Concentric structures from outside in: a thin outer brown 'Bark' layer; a very thin amber-coloured ring labelled 'Lateral meristem (cambium)' just inside the bark; multiple concentric rings of pale wood labelled 'Annual growth rings' with arrows pointing to wider rings labelled 'Wide ring — favourable year' and narrower rings labelled 'Narrow ring — dry year'; a small dark dot at the very centre labelled 'Pith'. Dark background (#0a0a0a). White labels with thin white leader lines. Soft browns and tans for wood, slight amber glow on the meristem ring to highlight it. Flat 2D textbook illustration style. No photorealism.
alt: Cross-section of a tree trunk showing concentric annual rings, with the lateral meristem ring labelled

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A wide grassy lawn at golden hour. In the left third, a manual lawn-mower has just passed through, leaving short cut grass. In the right two-thirds, fresh new green blades are visible pushing up from the cut bases. Soft warm sunset light angled across the scene. Painterly cinematic style, dark earthy palette, atmospheric haze, no text, no labels.
alt: A lawn being mowed in the foreground, with fresh green grass blades emerging from the cut bases

prompt: Educational botanical illustration of a single grass plant standing upright. The plant is shown from soil level to the top blade. Three distinct nodes (joint-like ridges) are visible along the stem, separated by smooth internodes. Each node is highlighted with a soft amber halo and labelled 'Intercalary meristem'. Two leaf blades emerge from these nodes. An inset close-up panel on the right shows a magnified view of the meristem zone at one node — small dividing cells with prominent nuclei. Labels: 'Node', 'Internode', 'Leaf base', 'Intercalary meristem (with arrow to inset)'. Dark background (#0a0a0a). White labels with thin white leader lines. Soft greens for the grass plant, warm amber accents on meristem zones. Flat 2D textbook illustration style. No photorealism.
alt: Diagram of a grass plant showing the location of intercalary meristems at the base of each node, with an inset close-up

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Macro photograph-style illustration of a single fresh green leaf, viewed from above. Round droplets of dew are scattered across its surface, beading rather than spreading because of an invisible waxy coating. Soft warm low-angle morning light catches the curves of the droplets. Painterly cinematic style, dark green and gold palette, atmospheric haze, no text, no labels.
alt: A close-up of a green leaf with a faint waxy sheen catching morning light, droplets of water beading on its surface

prompt: Cross-sectional botanical illustration of a leaf's outer surface, viewed from the side. Layers from top to bottom: a thin shiny waxy outer layer labelled 'Cuticle'; below it, a single row of flat rectangular cells tightly packed, labelled 'Epidermis'; below the epidermis, the inner mesophyll cells visible faintly. Two specialised features are highlighted: (1) a 'Stoma' — a tiny opening between two curved guard cells, with arrows showing CO2 entering and water vapour leaving; (2) an 'Epidermal hair' — a long single-celled projection sticking up from one of the epidermal cells. Dark background (#0a0a0a). White labels with thin white leader lines. Soft greens and turquoise for cells, slight amber for the cuticle. Flat 2D textbook illustration style. No photorealism.
alt: Cross-section of a leaf surface showing epidermis, cuticle, stomata, and an epidermal hair

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A young green plant stem photographed mid-bend in a strong wind, flexing into a graceful curve without snapping. Behind it, blurred grass blades caught in the same gust. Soft warm low-angle sunlight from the right. The image conveys flexibility, life, and resilience. Painterly cinematic style, dark green and amber palette, atmospheric haze, no text.
alt: A fresh green plant stem bending gracefully against the wind without breaking, showing both soft inner tissue and flexible outer regions
